assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, Section 2 ('Locate the Digest Comment') on lines 21-22 states: 'Search through all returned comments for those whose body starts with the marker string `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:`.'. The marker string matches exactly what is specified in shared/description-digest-protocol.md. Additionally, plan.md line 51 confirms: 'The digest comment is found with body `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:a1b2c3d4e5f67890...`'.

assertion_results.text: The response describes computing a digest of the current description (using scripts/sha256-digest.py or SHA-256) and comparing it with the stored digest
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, Section 5 ('Compute the Current Digest') on lines 48-54 describes: 'Extract the description field from the TC-9201 issue response. Write it to a temp file and compute the digest: python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/desc-TC-9201.txt'. Section 7 ('Compare Hex Digests') on lines 68-72 explicitly compares the stored hex digest with the computed hex digest. Section 6 ('Compare Format Tags') on lines 63-65 also describes comparing format tags before hex comparison.

assertion_results.text: The response confirms the digests match and states it will proceed silently without prompting the user — no additional latency for the happy path
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, Section 7 line 72 states: '**Result: MATCH.** The digests are identical, confirming that the task description has not been modified since plan-feature created it.' Section 8 ('Outcome') on line 76 states: 'Proceed silently to Step 2. No user prompt is needed, no warning is displayed, and no additional latency is introduced.' In plan.md line 51: '**Result: MATCH.** Proceed silently.'

assertion_results.text: The response does not alert the user or pause execution due to the digest check — it proceeds directly to subsequent steps
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-match.md, Section 8 ('Outcome') on line 76 explicitly states: 'Proceed silently to Step 2. No user prompt is needed, no warning is displayed, and no additional latency is introduced. The description integrity is verified.' The mismatch behavior (alerting user, pausing, asking to proceed or stop) is only described in the hypothetical 'What Would Happen on Mismatch' section (lines 79-88), which is clearly framed as a counterfactual ('If the hex digests had not matched, I would...'). In plan.md, Step 1.5 (line 51) confirms the match result and proceeds directly to Step 2 (line 55: 'The task has no dependencies. Proceed.').
